title: centaurea transcaucasica subsp. transcaucasica
intro: Centaurea transcaucasica subsp. transcaucasica, also known as Caucasus knapweed, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Caucasus region. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its flowers and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Centaurea transcaucasica subsp. transcaucasica include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting kidney function. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are commonly used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
